This method returns all active withdrawn reasons configured in the system.

The following considerations should be kept in mind when using this method:

  • Both system and user configured withdrawn reasons will appear in a unified list

","operationId":"Config_GetWithdrawnReason","consumes":[],"produces":["application/json","text/json","text/html","application/xml","text/xml"],"parameters":[{"name":"offset","in":"query","description":"Used for pagination. An offset to specify what page to start on for results. Used in conjunction with "rows".","required":false,"type":"integer","format":"int32"},{"name":"rows","in":"query","description":"Used for pagination. The number of rows to return on a dataset. The maximum value permitted is 100.","required":false,"type":"integer","format":"int32"}],"responses":{"200":{"description":"OK","schema":{"$ref":"#/definitions/WithdrawnReasonUIPaginationModel"}}},"deprecated":false},"post":{"tags":["Config"],"summary":"Add a new withdrawn reason ","description":"

This method will create (add) a new withdrawn reason to the portfolio. Once added, the withdrawn reason will immediately be available in the master list of withdrawn reasons.

The following considerations should be kept in mind when using this method:

  • Withdrawn reason Type will be either Company = 1 or Customer = 2
  • Duplicate withdrawn reasons cannot be added (A withdrawn reason with same name and type is considered Duplicate)
  • Withdrawn reason names cannot contain the following characters: < > =

Related Methods

  • List withdrawn reasons for a portfolio
  • Add a new withdrawn reason
  • Update a withdrawn reason name
","operationId":"Config_PostWithdrawnReason","consumes":["application/json","text/json","text/html","application/xml","text/xml","application/x-www-form-urlencoded"],"produces":["application/json","text/json","text/html","application/xml","text/xml"],"parameters":[{"name":"withdrawnReason","in":"body","description":"withdrawnReason post UI model","required":true,"schema":{"$ref":"#/definitions/WithdrawnReasonPostUIModel"}}],"responses":{"200":{"description":"OK","schema":{"$ref":"#/definitions/WithdrawnReasonUIModel"}}},"deprecated":false}},"/api/v1/config/withdrawnReasons/{withdrawnReasonId}":{"put":{"tags":["Config"],"summary":"Update a withdrawn reason ","description":"

This method will permit updating an existing withdrawn reason at the portfolio level. At this time, only the reason and visibility can be updated.

The following considerations should be kept in mind when using this method:

  • Updating (renaming) a withdrawn reason will rename it across the portfolio and the next time the withdrawn reason list is retrieved, the new name will be shown.
  • Withdrawn Reason Type will be either Company = 1 or Customer = 2
  • Withdrawn Reason type can be updated, if needed (Optional)
  • Duplicate withdrawn reasons cannot be added (A withdrawn reason with same name and type is considered Duplicate)
  • Withdrawn reason names cannot contain the following characters: < > =

Related Methods

  • List withdrawn reasons for a portfolio
  • Add a new withdrawn reason
","operationId":"Config_PutWithdrawnReason","consumes":["application/json","text/json","text/html","application/xml","text/xml","application/x-www-form-urlencoded"],"produces":[],"parameters":[{"name":"withdrawnReasonId","in":"path","description":"Unique withdrawnReason Id","required":true,"type":"integer","format":"int32"},{"name":"withdrawnReason","in":"body","description":"withdrawnReason put UI model","required":true,"schema":{"$ref":"#/definitions/WithdrawnReasonPutUIModel"}}],"responses":{"204":{"description":"No Content"}},"deprecated":false}},"/api/v1/customers":{"get":{"tags":["Customers"],"summary":"Returns every customer in the portfolio","description":"","operationId":"Customers_Get","consumes":[],"produces":["application/json","text/json","text/html","application/xml","text/xml"],"parameters":[{"name":"offset","in":"query","description":"Used for pagination. This method will create a customer record in the portfolio. This method is similar to using the new application page within the user interface to create a customer with no associated loan.

The following considerations should be kept in mind when using this method:

  • SSN and email address must be unique

Related Methods

  • Get customer
  • Update customer
  • Delete customer
","operationId":"Customers_Post","consumes":["application/json","text/json","text/html","application/xml","text/xml","application/x-www-form-urlencoded"],"produces":["application/json","text/json","text/html","application/xml","text/xml"],"parameters":[{"name":"customer","in":"body","description":"","required":true,"schema":{"$ref":"#/definitions/CustomerPostUIModel"}}],"responses":{"200":{"description":"OK","schema":{"$ref":"#/definitions/CustomerUIModel"}}},"deprecated":false}},"/api/v1/customers/{customerId}":{"get":{"tags":["Customers"],"summary":"Returns a customer object for the provided CustomerID","description":"","operationId":"Customers_GetById","consumes":[],"produces":["application/json","text/json","text/html","application/xml","text/xml"],"parameters":[{"name":"customerId","in":"path","description":"Unique Customer Id","required":true,"type":"integer","format":"int32"}],"responses":{"200":{"description":"OK","schema":{"$ref":"#/definitions/CustomerUIModel"}}},"deprecated":false},"put":{"tags":["Customers"],"summary":"Update existing customer","description":"","operationId":"Customers_Put","consumes":["application/json","text/json","text/html","application/xml","text/xml","application/x-www-form-urlencoded"],"produces":[],"parameters":[{"name":"customerId","in":"path","description":"Unique Customer Id","required":true,"type":"integer","format":"int32"},{"name":"customer","in":"body","description":"Customer Object","required":true,"schema":{"$ref":"#/definitions/CustomerUpdateModel"}}],"responses":{"204":{"description":"No Content"}},"deprecated":false},"delete":{"tags":["Customers"],"summary":"Delete a customer","description":"

This method will delete a customer record in the portfolio. This method has some restrictions when it can be used.

The following considerations should be kept in mind when using this method:

  • A customer can only be deleted if no loan has been created
  • A customer can only be deleted if no notes exist for the customer

Related Methods

  • Get customer
  • Update customer
  • Create customer

","operationId":"CustomFields_GetCustomFieldForCustomerById","consumes":[],"produces":["application/json","text/json","text/html","application/xml","text/xml"],"parameters":[{"name":"customerId","in":"path","description":"Unique Customer Id","required":true,"type":"integer","format":"int32"},{"name":"customFieldId","in":"path","description":"Unique CustomField Id","required":true,"type":"integer","format":"int32"}],"responses":{"200":{"description":"OK","schema":{"$ref":"#/definitions/CustomFieldUIModel"}}},"deprecated":false},"put":{"tags":["CustomFields"],"summary":"Update custom field","description":"
  • This method will permit updating a specific custom field for a particular customer
  • Maximum number of custom fields that can be created for a customer is 10
  • Only custom fields with Ids from 1 to 10 can be updated
  • Description field can be used to give the name or purpose of the custom field. It is optional field
  • Value field is a required field that holds the value of custom field. For example: description: "first name" , value: " Adam"
  • Custom field supports alphanumeric characters
  • Maximum number of characters allowed is 255

Returned from /loan","required":false,"type":"integer","format":"int32"},{"name":"documentType","in":"query","description":"Document type enumeration = ['1', '2', '3', '4', '99']","required":false,"type":"integer","format":"int32"},{"name":"documentDate","in":"query","description":"Document Created Date in Format YYYY-MM-DD","required":false,"type":"string"}],"responses":{"200":{"description":"OK","schema":{"$ref":"#/definitions/DocumentUIPaginationModel"}}},"deprecated":false},"post":{"tags":["Documents"],"summary":"Adds a new document (multipart/form-data; boundary)","description":"
Documents of any type can be uploaded and associated with a customer record (and optionally a specific loan). Each document requires a name to be provided along with the customer the document should be associated with.
Sometimes it is necessary to upload documents that are only intended to be viewed by agents and not customers. In this scenario, documents can be set as either private or not private with the isPrivate flag. This flag can be used when generating a list of documents where some need to be hidden from customers.
Uploading a document requires setting the proper authorization token in a header. Currently only documentType 99 (Other) can be set via API uploads.

Examples

Add a document

Upload a file called myagreement.pdf to the sandbox site for customer number 12345. Replace the token with a current active token.

curl --header "Authorization: Token d14653dcb3324d0dbbaf2738b70af079" -X POST -i -F parametername=@myagreement.pdf
https://api.sandbox.epicloansystems.com/api/v1/customers/12345/documents?documentName=myagreement.pdf&documentType=99


Add a document and associate with a particular loan

Upload a file called myagreement.pdf to the sandbox site for customer number 12345 and associate the document with loan 4567. Replace the token with a current active token.

curl --header "Authorization: Token d14653dcb3324d0dbbaf2738b70afing079" -X POST -i -F parametername=@myagreement.pdf
https://api.sandbox.epicloansystems.com/api/v1/customers/12345/documents?documentName=myagreement.pdf&documentType=99&loanId=4567


Add a document and set the privacy flag to true

Upload a file called myagreement.pdf to the sandbox site for customer number 12345 and set the document type to be other (99) and set the privacy flag to true. Replace the token with a current active token.

curl --header "Authorization: Token d14653dcb3324d0dbbaf2738b70af079" -X POST -i -F parametername=@myagreement.pdf
https://api.sandbox.epicloansystems.com/api/v1/customers/12345/documents?documentName=myagreement.pdf&documentType=99&isPrivate=true

This method will return a list of flags currently enabled for a specific customer.

The following considerations should be kept in mind when using this method:

  • The list of flags returned are only enabled flags for this customer
  • The flagActivationDate represents the date and time the flag was enabled for this customer
","operationId":"Flags_GetCustomerFlags","consumes":[],"produces":["application/json","text/json","text/html","application/xml","text/xml"],"parameters":[{"name":"customerId","in":"path","description":"Unique Customer Id","required":true,"type":"integer","format":"int32"},{"name":"priority","in":"query","description":"Flag priority","required":false,"type":"integer","format":"int32"},{"name":"offset","in":"query","description":"Used for pagination. The maximum value permitted is 100.","required":false,"type":"integer","format":"int32"}],"responses":{"200":{"description":"OK","schema":{"$ref":"#/definitions/CustomerFlagPaginationModel"}}},"deprecated":false}},"/api/v1/customers/{customerId}/flags/{flagId}":{"put":{"tags":["Flags"],"summary":"Update customer flag","description":"

This method will permit toggling the status of a specific flag for a customer. This can be used to keep track of activities that have been completed for a customer.

The following considerations should be kept in mind when using this method:

  • The master list of flags that can be set are pulled from the master list of flags enabled on the portfolio
  • The flag status is either enabled or disabled
  • When the status is enabled, the system will automatically record the transition date and time in the flagActivationDate field
  • Toggling the flag status only affects the current customer

This method provides the ability to edit a note created by an employee. This method will not permit editing system notes.

The following considerations should be kept in mind when using this method:

  • Identifying a note requires matching both the customerId and the noteDate exactly. The noteDate can be obtained via the GET /api/v1/customers/{customerId}/notes method.
  • Only the note body and note subject can be modified
  • Editing a note is a permanent non-recoverable action; previous content will be erased and replaced with what is provided
  • Only agent (employee) notes can be edited

Related Methods

  • Add a note
  • List notes
  • Delete a note
","operationId":"Notes_PutNote","consumes":["application/json","text/json","text/html","application/xml","text/xml","application/x-www-form-urlencoded"],"produces":[],"parameters":[{"name":"customerId","in":"path","description":"Unique Customer Id","required":true,"type":"integer","format":"int32"},{"name":"noteDate","in":"query","description":"Unique Note Date yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.fffffffZ","required":true,"type":"string"},{"name":"note","in":"body","description":"Note Update Model","required":true,"schema":{"$ref":"#/definitions/NotePutUIModel"}}],"responses":{"204":{"description":"No Content"}},"deprecated":false},"post":{"tags":["Notes"],"summary":"Adds a new note","description":"","operationId":"Notes_Post","consumes":["application/json","text/json","text/html","application/xml","text/xml","application/x-www-form-urlencoded"],"produces":["application/json","text/json","text/html","application/xml","text/xml"],"parameters":[{"name":"customerId","in":"path","description":"Unique Customer Id","required":true,"type":"integer","format":"int32"},{"name":"note","in":"body","description":"","required":true,"schema":{"$ref":"#/definitions/NoteUIPostModel"}}],"responses":{"200":{"description":"OK","schema":{"$ref":"#/definitions/Object"}}},"deprecated":false},"delete":{"tags":["Notes"],"summary":"Delete a note","description":"

This method provides the ability to delete a note created by an employee. This method will not permit deleting system notes.

The following considerations should be kept in mind when using this method:

  • Deleting a note is a permanent non-recoverable action
  • Only agent (employee) notes can be deleted

Related Methods

  • Add a note
  • List notes
  • Edit a note
","operationId":"Notes_DeleteAgentNote","consumes":[],"produces":[],"parameters":[{"name":"customerId","in":"path","description":"Unique Customer Id","required":true,"type":"integer","format":"int32"},{"name":"noteDate","in":"query","description":"Unique Note Date yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.fffffffZ","required":true,"type":"string"}],"responses":{"204":{"description":"No Content"}},"deprecated":false}},"/api/v1/customers/{customerId}/payrolls":{"get":{"tags":["Payroll"],"summary":"Returns payroll information for a customer","description":"","operationId":"Payroll_GetPayrollForCustomer","consumes":[],"produces":["application/json","text/json","text/html","application/xml","text/xml"],"parameters":[{"name":"customerId","in":"path","description":"Unique Customer Id","required":true,"type":"integer","format":"int32"},{"name":"isPrimary","in":"query","description":"Indicates if the payroll record information retrieved is the primary record information","required":false,"type":"boolean"}],"responses":{"200":{"description":"OK","schema":{"type":"array","items":{"$ref":"#/definitions/PayrollUIModel"}}}},"deprecated":false},"post":{"tags":["Payroll"],"summary":"Adds a new payroll information record","description":"

When a POST occurs, a payroll JSON structure should be submitted with all the required fields. All fields other than payrollId, netMonthlyIncome, payDateNext, and payDateSecondNext are required fields. A different JSON structure (subset of the master fields) needs to be passed in depending on the values for frequency and frequencySubType. See examples below for specifics related to each frequency type. When isPrimary is true, the system will automatically make the current record primary and will remove the isPrimary=true status from all previous records. If a record is submitted and it is the ONLY payroll record, the API will automatically set this record as primary. This is to fulfill the requirement that one record exists that is primary.

Examples

Daily Frequency Post Structure
EXAMPLE UPDATE STRUCTURE

                              {
                                  "payroll":
                                  {
                                  "isPrimary": true,
                                  "payrollType": 2,
                                  "totalPayCheck": 543.55,
                                  "grossMonthlyIncome": 695.33,
                                  "lastPayrollDate": "2017-03-03",
                                  "isMoveToNextBusinessDay": false,
                                  "frequency": 5					// Indicates daily
                                  }
                              }
Weekly Frequency Post Structure

FIELDS USED

dayofweek

EXAMPLE UPDATE STRUCTURE

                               {
                                  "payroll": 
                                  {
                                     "isPrimary": true,
                                     "payrollType": 2,
                                     "totalPayCheck": 543.55,
                                     "grossMonthlyIncome": 695.33,
                                     "lastPayrollDate": "2017-03-03",
                                     "isMoveToNextBusinessDay": false,
                                     "frequency": 1,					// Indicates weekly
                                     "frequencySubType": 10,
                                     "payroll_details": [
                                         {
                                          "dayofweek": 4			// Only the dayofweek field is used
                                        }
                                     ]
                                  }
                                }
Bi-Weekly Frequency Post Structure

FIELDS USED

dayofweek

EXAMPLE UPDATE STRUCTURE

                              {
                                "payroll": {
                                  "isPrimary": true,
                                  "payrollType": 2,
                                  "totalPayCheck": 543.55,
                                  "grossMonthlyIncome": 695.33,
                                  "lastPayrollDate": "2017-03-03",
                                  "isMoveToNextBusinessDay": false,
                                  "frequency": 2,					// Indicates bi-weekly
                                  "frequencySubType": 20,
                                  "payroll_details": [
                                    {
                                    "dayofweek": 4			// Only the dayofweek field is used
                                    }
                                  ]
                                }
                              }
Semi-Monthly Frequency Post Structure – Specific Two Days of Month

FIELDS USED

dayofmonth

EXAMPLE STRUCTURE

IMPORTANT NOTE: In the case of semi-monthly where two dates are explicitly defined, the payroll_details block must be listed for each date. 
                                {
                                  "payroll": {
                                    "isPrimary": true,
                                    "payrollType": 2,
                                    "totalPayCheck": 543.55,
                                    "grossMonthlyIncome": 695.33,
                                    "lastPayrollDate": "2017-03-03",
                                    "isMoveToNextBusinessDay": false,
                                    "frequency": 3,			// Indicates semi-monthly
                                    "frequencySubType": 30,
                                    "payroll_details": [
                                      {
                                        "dayofmonth": 4	// Only the dayofmonth field is used
                                      },
                                      {
                                        "dayofmonth": 20	// Only the dayofmonth field is used
                                      }
                                    ]
                                  }
                                }
Semi-Monthly Frequency Post Structure – Relative Two Days of Month

FIELDS USED

dayofmonth

EXAMPLE STRUCTURE

IMPORTANT NOTE: In the case of semi-monthly where two dates are explicitly defined, the payroll_details block must be listed for each date. 
                                {
                                  "payroll": {
                                    "isPrimary": true,
                                    "payrollType": 2,
                                    "totalPayCheck": 543.55,
                                    "grossMonthlyIncome": 695.33,
                                    "lastPayrollDate": "2017-03-03",
                                    "isMoveToNextBusinessDay": false,
                                    "frequency": 3,				// Indicates semi-monthly
                                    "frequencySubType": 31,
                                    "payroll_details": [
                                      {
                                        "dayofweek": "",		// Only the dayofweek field is used
                                        "whichweek": ""			// Only the whichweek field is used
                                      },
                                      {
                                        "dayofweek": "",		// Only the dayofweek field is used
                                        "whichweek": ""			// Only the whichweek field is used
                                      }
                                    ]
                                  }
                                }
Monthly Frequency Post Structure – Specific Day of Month

FIELDS USED

dayofmonth

EXAMPLE STRUCTURE

                                {
                                  "payroll": {
                                    "isPrimary": true,
                                    "payrollType": 2,
                                    "totalPayCheck": 543.55,
                                    "grossMonthlyIncome": 695.33,
                                    "lastPayrollDate": "2017-03-03",
                                    "isMoveToNextBusinessDay": false,
                                    "frequency": 4,				// Indicates monthly
                                    "frequencySubType": 40,
                                    "payroll_details": [
                                      {
                                        "dayofmonth": 15		// Only the dayofmonth field is used
                                      }
                                    ]
                                  }
                                }
Monthly Frequency Post Structure – Relative Day of Month

FIELDS USED

dayofweek
whichweek

EXAMPLE STRUCTURE

                                {
                                  "payroll": {
                                    "isPrimary": true,
                                    "payrollType": 2,
                                    "totalPayCheck": 543.55,
                                    "grossMonthlyIncome": 695.33,
                                    "lastPayrollDate": "2017-03-03",
                                    "isMoveToNextBusinessDay": false,
                                    "frequency": 4,				// Indicates monthly
                                    "frequencySubType": 41,
                                    "payroll_details": [
                                      {
                                        "dayofweek": "",		// Only the dayofweek field is used
                                        "whichweek": ""			// Only the whichweek field is used
                                      }
                                    ]
                                  }
                                }
Monthly Frequency Post Structure – Day of Month After a Relative Day

FIELDS USED

dayofweek
dayofmonthafter

EXAMPLE UPDATE STRUCTURE

                                {
                                  "payroll": {
                                    "isPrimary": true,
                                    "payrollType": 2,
                                    "totalPayCheck": 543.55,
                                    "grossMonthlyIncome": 695.33,
                                    "lastPayrollDate": "2017-03-03",
                                    "isMoveToNextBusinessDay": false,
                                    "frequency": 4,					// Indicates monthly
                                    "frequencySubType": 42,
                                    "payroll_details": [
                                      {
                                        "dayofweek": "",			// Only the dayofweek field is used
                                        "dayofmonthafter": ""		// Only the dayofmonthafter field is used
                                      }
                                    ]
                                  }
                                }
","operationId":"Payroll_PostPayroll","consumes":["application/json","text/json","text/html","application/xml","text/xml","application/x-www-form-urlencoded"],"produces":["application/json","text/json","text/html","application/xml","text/xml"],"parameters":[{"name":"customerId","in":"path","description":"Unique Customer Id","required":true,"type":"integer","format":"int32"},{"name":"payrollInformation","in":"body","description":"Payroll record in Json format","required":true,"schema":{"$ref":"#/definitions/PayrollPostUIModel"}}],"responses":{"200":{"description":"OK","schema":{"$ref":"#/definitions/PayrollUIModel"}}},"deprecated":false}},"/api/v1/customers/{customerId}/payrolls/{payrollId}":{"get":{"tags":["Payroll"],"summary":"Returns a payroll object for a specific payrollId","description":"","operationId":"Payroll_GetByPayrollIdForCustomer","consumes":[],"produces":["application/json","text/json","text/html","application/xml","text/xml"],"parameters":[{"name":"customerId","in":"path","description":"Unique Customer Id","required":true,"type":"integer","format":"int32"},{"name":"payrollId","in":"path","description":"Unique Payroll Id","required":true,"type":"integer","format":"int32"}],"responses":{"200":{"description":"OK","schema":{"$ref":"#/definitions/PayrollUIModel"}}},"deprecated":false},"put":{"tags":["Payroll"],"summary":"Update payroll information for a customer","description":"

When a PUT occurs, a payroll JSON structure can be submitted for the entire payrolls structure or only the fields needing to be changed can be submitted. Read-only fields cannot be updated. When updating any element related to frequency and frequencySubType the payroll_details array needs to be submitted also. See examples below for specifics related to each frequency type.

Examples

Daily Frequency Update Structure
EXAMPLE UPDATE STRUCTURE

                          {
                              "payroll": 
                              {
                              "isPrimary": true,
                              "payrollType": 2,
                              "totalPayCheck": 543.55,
                              "grossMonthlyIncome": 695.33,
                              "lastPayrollDate": "2017-03-03",
                              "isMoveToNextBusinessDay": false,
                              "frequency": 5					// Indicates Daily
                              }
                          }
Weekly Frequency Update Structure

FIELDS USED

dayofweek

EXAMPLE UPDATE STRUCTURE

                          {
                            "payroll": {
                              "isPrimary": true,
                              "payrollType": 2,
                              "totalPayCheck": 543.55,
                              "grossMonthlyIncome": 695.33,
                              "lastPayrollDate": "2017-03-03",
                              "isMoveToNextBusinessDay": false,
                              "frequency": 1,					// Indicates weekly
                              "frequencySubType": 10,
                              "payroll_details": [
                                {
                                  "dayofweek": 4			// Only the dayofweek field is used
                                }
                              ]
                            }
                          }
Bi-Weekly Frequency Update Structure

FIELDS USED

dayofweek

EXAMPLE STRUCTURE

                          {
                            "payroll": {
                              "isPrimary": true,
                              "payrollType": 2,
                              "totalPayCheck": 543.55,
                              "grossMonthlyIncome": 695.3,
                              "lastPayrollDate": "2017-03-03",
                              "isMoveToNextBusinessDay": false,
                              "frequency": 2,					// Indicates bi-weekly
                              "frequencySubType": 20,
                              "payroll_details": [
                                {
                                  "dayofweek": 4			// Only the dayofweek field is used
                                }
                              ]
                            }
                          }
Semi-Monthly Frequency Update Structure – Specific Two Days of Month

FIELDS USED

dayofmonth

EXAMPLE STRUCTURE

IMPORTANT NOTE: In the case of semi-monthly where two dates are explicitly defined, the payroll_details block must be listed for each date. 
                          {
                            "payroll": {
                              "isPrimary": true,
                              "payrollType": 2,
                              "totalPayCheck": 543.55,
                              "grossMonthlyIncome": 695.33,
                              "lastPayrollDate": "2017-03-03",
                              "isMoveToNextBusinessDay": false,
                              "frequency": 3,			// Indicates semi-monthly
                              "frequencySubType": 30,
                              "payroll_details": [
                                {
                                  "dayofmonth": 4	// Only the dayofmonth field is used
                                },
                                {
                                  "dayofmonth": 20	// Only the dayofmonth field is used
                                }
                              ]
                            }
                          }
Semi-Monthly Frequency Update Structure – Relative Two Days of Month

FIELDS USED

dayofmonth

EXAMPLE STRUCTURE

IMPORTANT NOTE: In the case of semi-monthly where two dates are explicitly defined, the payroll_details block must be listed for each date. 
                          {
                            "payroll": {
                              "isPrimary": true,
                              "payrollType": 2,
                              "totalPayCheck": 543.55,
                              "grossMonthlyIncome": 695.33,
                              "lastPayrollDate": "2017-03-03",
                              "isMoveToNextBusinessDay": false,
                              "frequency": 3,				// Indicates semi-monthly
                              "frequencySubType": 31,
                              "payroll_details": [
                                {
                                  "dayofweek": "",		// Only the dayofweek field is used
                                  "whichweek": ""			// Only the whichweek field is used
                                },
                                {
                                  "dayofweek": "",		// Only the dayofweek field is used
                                  "whichweek": ""			// Only the whichweek field is used
                                }
                              ]
                            }
                          }
Monthly Frequency Update Structure – Specific Day of Month

FIELDS USED

dayofmonth

EXAMPLE STRUCTURE

                          {
                            "payroll": {
                              "isPrimary": true,
                              "payrollType": 2,
                              "totalPayCheck": 543.55,
                              "grossMonthlyIncome": 695.33,
                              "lastPayrollDate": "2017-03-03",
                              "isMoveToNextBusinessDay": false,
                              "frequency": 4,				// Indicates monthly
                              "frequencySubType": 40,
                              "payroll_details": [
                                {
                                  "dayofmonth": 15		// Only the dayofmonth field is used
                                }
                              ]
                            }
                          }
Monthly Frequency Update Structure – Relative Day of Month

FIELDS USED

dayofweek
whichweek

EXAMPLE STRUCTURE

                          {
                            "payroll": {
                              "isPrimary": true,
                              "payrollType": 2,
                              "totalPayCheck": 543.55,
                              "grossMonthlyIncome": 695.33,
                              "lastPayrollDate": "2017-03-03",
                              "isMoveToNextBusinessDay": false,
                              "frequency": 4,				// Indicates monthly
                              "frequencySubType": 41,
                              "payroll_details": [
                                {
                                  "dayofweek": "",		// Only the dayofweek field is used
                                  "whichweek": ""			// Only the whichweek field is used
                                }
                              ]
                            }
                          }
Monthly Frequency Update Structure – Day of Month After a Relative Day

FIELDS USED

dayofweek
dayofmonthafter

EXAMPLE UPDATE STRUCTURE

                          {
                            "payroll": {
                              "isPrimary": true,
                              "payrollType": 2,
                              "totalPayCheck": 543.55,
                              "grossMonthlyIncome": 695.33,
                              "lastPayrollDate": "2017-03-03",
                              "isMoveToNextBusinessDay": false,
                              "frequency": 4,					// Indicates monthly
                              "frequencySubType": 42,
                              "payroll_details": [
                                {
                                  "dayofweek": "",			// Only the dayofweek field is used
                                  "dayofmonthafter": ""		// Only the dayofmonthafter field is used
                                }
                              ]
                            }
                          }

This method will return a list of references for a particular customer.

Related Methods

  • Update Reference
  • Create Reference
  • Delete Reference
","operationId":"References_GetCustomerReferences","consumes":[],"produces":["application/json","text/json","text/html","application/xml","text/xml"],"parameters":[{"name":"customerId","in":"path","description":"Unique customer Id","required":true,"type":"integer","format":"int32"},{"name":"offset","in":"query","description":"Used for pagination. Used in conjunction with "rows".","required":false,"type":"integer","format":"int32"},{"name":"rows","in":"query","description":"Used for pagination. The number of rows to return on a dataset. The maximum value permitted is 100.","required":false,"type":"integer","format":"int32"}],"responses":{"200":{"description":"OK","schema":{"$ref":"#/definitions/CustomerReferencePaginationModel"}}},"deprecated":false},"post":{"tags":["References"],"summary":"Create a reference","description":"

This method will create (add) a new reference for a particular customer. Once added, the reference will immediately be available in the master list of references for that customer.

The following considerations should be kept in mind when using this method:

  • Maximum number of references that can be added per customer is 10.
  • By default the first reference added will be marked as primary.

Related Methods

  • Update Reference
  • List Reference
  • Delete Reference
","operationId":"References_PostCustomerReference","consumes":["application/json","text/json","text/html","application/xml","text/xml","application/x-www-form-urlencoded"],"produces":["application/json","text/json","text/html","application/xml","text/xml"],"parameters":[{"name":"customerId","in":"path","description":"Unique customer Id","required":true,"type":"integer","format":"int32"},{"name":"customerReference","in":"body","description":"Customer reference in json format","required":true,"schema":{"$ref":"#/definitions/CustomerReferencePostUIModel"}}],"responses":{"200":{"description":"OK","schema":{"$ref":"#/definitions/CustomerReferenceUIModel"}}},"deprecated":false}},"/api/v1/customers/{customerId}/references/{referenceId}":{"put":{"tags":["References"],"summary":"Update a reference","description":"

This method will permit updating a specific reference for a particular customer.

The following considerations should be kept in mind when using this method:

  • If there is only 1 reference existing for a customer, then isPrimaryReference cannot be updated to false
  • To update a primary reference to non-primary, you must create another reference and set it to primary or update an existing reference to be the primary.

Related Methods

  • List Reference
  • Create Reference
  • Delete Reference
","operationId":"References_PutCustomerReference","consumes":["application/json","text/json","text/html","application/xml","text/xml","application/x-www-form-urlencoded"],"produces":[],"parameters":[{"name":"customerId","in":"path","description":"Unique customer Id","required":true,"type":"integer","format":"int32"},{"name":"referenceId","in":"path","description":"Unique reference Id","required":true,"type":"integer","format":"int32"},{"name":"customerReference","in":"body","description":"Customer reference in json format","required":true,"schema":{"$ref":"#/definitions/CustomerReferencePutUIModel"}}],"responses":{"204":{"description":"No Content"}},"deprecated":false},"delete":{"tags":["References"],"summary":"Delete a reference","description":"

This method will remove a reference from a customer. Once removed the reference will no longer appear in the list of references on customer record.

If the deleted reference was a primary reference then another reference (if one exists) will be marked as primary.

","operationId":"References_DeleteCustomerReference","consumes":[],"produces":[],"parameters":[{"name":"customerId","in":"path","description":"Unique customer Id","required":true,"type":"integer","format":"int32"},{"name":"referenceId","in":"path","description":"Unique customer reference ID","required":true,"type":"integer","format":"int32"}],"responses":{"204":{"description":"No Content"}},"deprecated":false}},"/api/v1/customers/{customerId}/underwriting/ibvStatus/{providerId}":{"get":{"tags":["Underwriting"],"summary":"List customer IBV status","description":"

For portfolios which have IBV underwriting enabled this method will return the latest IBV status of a particular customer for a given IBV provider Id. IBV status listed for a customer via this method will return the reference Id, request created date, request status, status change date, error message and provider Id.

The following considerations should be kept in mind when using this method:

  • The only supported IBV provider at this time is DecisionLogic and its corresponding provider id is "1".
  • "ReferenceId" corresponds to request code for DecisionLogic.
  • "RequestCreatedDate" is the date and time when the request is created in EPIC.
  • "IbvRequestStatus" is the status of the IBV request in EPIC. Valid statuses are 1 = NotStarted, 2 = InProcess, 3 = Ready, 4 = Error and 5 = Warning.
  • "StatusChangeDate" is the date and time when the status of the IBV request is changed after request is initiated. This applies to status changes from InProcess to Ready or InProcess to Error or In Process to Warning.
  • "Error " will display a detailed error message as received by provider, in cases when IBV request results in error.

","operationId":"Underwriting_GetRequestStatus","consumes":[],"produces":["application/json","text/json","text/html","application/xml","text/xml"],"parameters":[{"name":"customerId","in":"path","description":"Unique customer Id","required":true,"type":"integer","format":"int32"},{"name":"providerId","in":"path","description":"IBV provider Id","required":true,"type":"integer","format":"int32"}],"responses":{"200":{"description":"OK","schema":{"$ref":"#/definitions/IbvRequestStatusUIModel"}}},"deprecated":false}},"/api/v1/customers/{customerId}/UpdateACHRevokeStatus":{"put":{"tags":["Customers"],"summary":"Update the ACH revoke status for a specific customer","description":"

This method provides the ability to update the ACH Revoke/Authorize ACH status for a specific customer.

The following considerations should be kept in mind:

  • This endpoint can only be used if the ACH Revoke/Authorize ACH feature is enabled in the LMS
  • This endpoint will not update the ACH Revoke/Authorize ACH status for any other customers that share the same bank account information (ABA/Bank Account number)
","operationId":"Customers_UpdateACHRevokeStatus","consumes":[],"produces":[],"parameters":[{"name":"customerId","in":"path","description":"Customer ID.","required":true,"type":"integer","format":"int32"},{"name":"achAuthorization","in":"query","description":"0 to authorize the customer's ACH account; 1 to revoke the customer's ACH account.","required":true,"type":"integer","format":"int32"},{"name":"employeeId","in":"query","description":"Employee ID.","required":false,"type":"integer","format":"int32"}],"responses":{"204":{"description":"No Content"}},"deprecated":false}},"/api/v1/customers/UpdateACHRevokeStatus":{"put":{"tags":["Customers"],"summary":"Update the ACH revoke status for all customers with the same bank account information.","description":"

This method provides the ability to update the ACH Revoke/Authorize ACH status for all customers that share the same bank account information (ABA/Bank Account number).

The following considerations should be kept in mind:

  • This endpoint can only be used if the ACH Revoke/Authorize ACH by Customer feature is not enabled in the LMS.
  • If this endpoint is used and the ACH Revoke/Authorize ACH by Customer feature is enabled an error message will display, and the update will not be allowed.
","operationId":"Customers_UpdateSameInfoACHRevokeStatus","consumes":[],"produces":[],"parameters":[{"name":"routingNumber","in":"query","description":"ABA/routing Number..","required":true,"type":"string"},{"name":"bankAccountNumber","in":"query","description":"Bank account Number.","required":true,"type":"string"},{"name":"achAuthorization","in":"query","description":"0 to authorize the customer's ACH account; 1 to revoke the customer's ACH account.","required":true,"type":"integer","format":"int32"},{"name":"employeeId","in":"query","description":"Employee ID.","required":false,"type":"integer","format":"int32"}],"responses":{"204":{"description":"No Content"}},"deprecated":false}},"/api/v1/flags":{"get":{"tags":["Flags"],"summary":"List flags for a portfolio","description":"

This method will return a list of flags configured in the system at the portfolio level. Flags listed via this method will return the flagId and the flagName.

Related Methods

  • Add a new flag
  • Delete a flag
  • Update a flag name
","operationId":"Flags_GetPortfolioFlags","consumes":[],"produces":["application/json","text/json","text/html","application/xml","text/xml"],"parameters":[{"name":"offset","in":"query","description":"Used for pagination. An offset to specify what page to start on for results. Used in conjunction with "rows".","required":false,"type":"integer","format":"int32"},{"name":"rows","in":"query","description":"Used for pagination. The number of rows to return on a dataset. The maximum value permitted is 100.","required":false,"type":"integer","format":"int32"}],"responses":{"200":{"description":"OK","schema":{"$ref":"#/definitions/FlagDefinitionPaginationModel"}}},"deprecated":false},"post":{"tags":["Flags"],"summary":"Add a new flag ","description":"

This method will create (add) a new flag to the portfolio. Once added, the flag will immediately be available in the master list of flags and on customer and loan flag lists w.r.t. the flag type.

The following considerations should be kept in mind when using this method:

  • Flag names have a maximum length of 100 characters
  • Flag Type will be either Customer = 1 or Loan = 2
  • Flag Priority will be either Normal= 3 or High = 1
  • Duplicate flags cannot be added (A Flag with same name and type is considered Duplicate)
  • Flag names cannot contain the following characters: < > =
  • Flag Type once added can't be modified

Related Methods

  • List flags for a portfolio
  • Add a new flag
  • Delete a flag
  • Update a flag name
","operationId":"Flags_PostFlag","consumes":["application/json","text/json","text/html","application/xml","text/xml","application/x-www-form-urlencoded"],"produces":["application/json","text/json","text/html","application/xml","text/xml"],"parameters":[{"name":"flag","in":"body","description":"flag post UI model","required":true,"schema":{"$ref":"#/definitions/FlagPostUIModel"}}],"responses":{"200":{"description":"OK","schema":{"$ref":"#/definitions/FlagDefinitionUIModel"}}},"deprecated":false}},"/api/v1/flags/{flagId}":{"put":{"tags":["Flags"],"summary":"Update a flag name ","description":"

This method will permit updating an existing flag at the portfolio level. At this time, only the flagName and priority can be the update action.

The following considerations should be kept in mind when using this method:

  • Updating (renaming) a flagName will rename it across the portfolio and the next time a flag list for a customer/loan is retrieved, the new name will be shown.
  • Flag Priority can be updated to either Normal= 3 or High = 1
  • Flag Type once added can't be modified
  • Flag names have a maximum length of 100 characters
  • Duplicate flags cannot be added (A Flag with same name and type is considered Duplicate)
  • Flag names cannot contain the following characters: < > =

Related Methods

  • List flags for a portfolio
  • Add a new flag
  • Delete a flag
","operationId":"Flags_PutFlag","consumes":["application/json","text/json","text/html","application/xml","text/xml","application/x-www-form-urlencoded"],"produces":[],"parameters":[{"name":"flagId","in":"path","description":"Unique flag Id","required":true,"type":"integer","format":"int32"},{"name":"flag","in":"body","description":"flag put UI model","required":true,"schema":{"$ref":"#/definitions/FlagPutUIModel"}}],"responses":{"204":{"description":"No Content"}},"deprecated":false},"delete":{"tags":["Flags"],"summary":"Delete a flag","description":"

This method will remove a flag from a portfolio. This method operates at the portfolio level and once removed the flag will no longer appear in the master list of flags nor on any customer record.

The following considerations should be kept in mind when using this method:

  • When a flag is removed, it cannot be recovered. All prior values associated with a flag on any customer record will be lost.

Related Methods

  • List flags for a portfolio
  • Add a new flag
  • Update a flag name
","operationId":"Flags_DeleteFlag","consumes":[],"produces":[],"parameters":[{"name":"flagId","in":"path","description":"Unique flag Id","required":true,"type":"integer","format":"int32"}],"responses":{"204":{"description":"No Content"}},"deprecated":false}},"/api/v1/leadTokens/leadTokenId":{"get":{"tags":["Leads"],"summary":"Returns customer and loan Id's for a lead token","description":"

This method will return details about a lead accepted into the system when a leadTokenId is available. By passing the leadTokenId into this method, it will currently return back a JSON structure with the customerId and loanId associated with the accepted lead. This is useful if external systems want to correlate a lead sent into the system and know the final specific records created within the EPIC LMS.

The JSON response structure returned from a valid leadTokenId is: 

                  {
                    "customerId" : "12345",
                    "loanId" : "42313"
                  }

The following considerations should be kept in mind when using this method:

  • For security reasons, a leadTokenId can only display information for the same portfolio from which it was originally generated

Must be a single date in the format yyyy-MM-dd","required":false,"type":"string"}],"responses":{"200":{"description":"OK","schema":{"$ref":"#/definitions/LoanUIPaginationModel"}}},"deprecated":false}},"/api/v1/loans/{customerId}/expressApp":{"post":{"tags":["Loans"],"summary":"Create Express Loan ","description":"

This endpoint provides the ability to create an express application for an existing customer:

The following considerations should be kept in mind while using this method:

  • Express application can be denied by internal or external underwriting.
  • CustomerId, must exist in the system.
  • StoreId, ProgamId and ProviderId must be active.
  • The provided Program must belong to the provided Store.
  • The provided Program must be configured to support the Client's Payroll Frequency.
  • Requested Loan Amount needs to fall between the Min and Max Loan Amount configured in both Program and Store.

The Effective Date will automatically adjust to next business day in the following scenarios:

  • The Effective Date is today's date.
  • The Effective Date is in the past.
  • The Effective Date falls on weekend.
  • The Effective Date Cutoff time has been reached.
","operationId":"Loans_CreateLoanExpress","consumes":["application/json","text/json","text/html","application/xml","text/xml","application/x-www-form-urlencoded"],"produces":["application/json","text/json","text/html","application/xml","text/xml"],"parameters":[{"name":"customerId","in":"path","description":"Unique customer Id","required":true,"type":"integer","format":"int32"},{"name":"loanExpress","in":"body","description":"loanExpress post UI model","required":true,"schema":{"$ref":"#/definitions/LoanExpressUIModel"}}],"responses":{"200":{"description":"OK","schema":{"$ref":"#/definitions/LoanExpressUIResponseModel"}}},"deprecated":false}},"/api/v1/loans/{loanId}":{"get":{"tags":["Loans"],"summary":"Returns a loan object for the provided LoanID","description":"","operationId":"Loans_GetLoanById","consumes":[],"produces":["application/json","text/json","text/html","application/xml","text/xml"],"parameters":[{"name":"loanId","in":"path","description":"Unique loan ID. Returned from /loans","required":true,"type":"integer","format":"int32"}],"responses":{"200":{"description":"OK","schema":{"$ref":"#/definitions/LoanUIModel"}}},"deprecated":false}},"/api/v1/loans/{loanId}/agent":{"get":{"tags":["Loans"],"summary":"List agent assignments on a loan","description":"

This method will return employee assignment information on a loan. The response JSON structure will return the assigned loan manager and the assigned collector agent for the specified loan.

The following considerations should be kept in mind when using this method:

  • When no agent is assigned a null value will be returned
  • assignedLoanManagerId is applicable for both loans in good standing as well as loans in collection
  • assignedCollectorId is only applicable for loans in collection

Related Methods

  • Update current employee assignments on a loan
  • Get employees
","operationId":"Loans_GetAssignedEmployees","consumes":[],"produces":["application/json","text/json","text/html","application/xml","text/xml"],"parameters":[{"name":"loanId","in":"path","description":"Unique loan ID. Returned from /loan","required":true,"type":"integer","format":"int32"}],"responses":{"200":{"description":"OK","schema":{"$ref":"#/definitions/AssignedEmployeesUIModel"}}},"deprecated":false},"put":{"tags":["Loans"],"summary":"Update current employee assignments on a loan","description":"

This method will permit changing the loan manager or assigned collector on a loan. This method accepts an agent identifier and will determine the correct assignment to update based on the current status of the loan. By default, attempting to assign an agent to a loan that already has an agent assigned will fail. An override option is available (forceAssignment) to force a change on a loan with an agent currently assigned.

When an assignment request is made, the system will use the following rules to determine which agent assignment type to update:

  • Loans in pending status will update the assignedLoanManagerId field
  • Loans in "active collections" status will update the assignedCollectorId field

The following loan statuses are considered "active collections":

  • Returned Item (status = 37)
  • Charged Off (status = 16)
  • Returned Item Pending Paid Off (status = 30)
  • Charged Off Pending Paid Off (status = 32)

The following considerations should be kept in mind when using this method:

  • Only Pending Applications or Loans in Active Collections status can be updated.
  • An employee must be in a collections department to be assigned as the assignedCollectorId when a loan is in an "active collections" status.
  • Loans in pending application status can be set to "Not Assigned" by passing -1 as the value for assignedAgentId.
  • Attempting to assign an agent to a loan with an agent already assigned will fail unless the forceAssignment parameter is set to true.

Related Methods

  • List current employee assignments on a loan
  • Get employees
  • Get departments

Returned from /loan","required":true,"type":"integer","format":"int32"},{"name":"loanAssignmentPutUiModel","in":"body","description":"","required":true,"schema":{"$ref":"#/definitions/LoanAssignmentPutUIModel"}}],"responses":{"204":{"description":"No Content"}},"deprecated":false}},"/api/v1/loans/{loanId}/approve":{"put":{"tags":["Loans"],"summary":"Approve a loan ","description":"

This method provides the ability to approve a loan.

The following considerations should be kept in mind when using this method:

  • A loan that is to be approved must be originated and in Pending Application status.

","operationId":"Loans_ApproveLoan","consumes":["application/json","text/json","text/html","application/xml","text/xml","application/x-www-form-urlencoded"],"produces":[],"parameters":[{"name":"loanId","in":"path","description":"Unique loan Id. Returned from /loan","required":true,"type":"integer","format":"int32"},{"name":"approveModel","in":"body","description":"","required":true,"schema":{"$ref":"#/definitions/LoanOriginationUIModel"}}],"responses":{"204":{"description":"No Content"}},"deprecated":false}},"/api/v1/loans/{loanId}/balance":{"get":{"tags":["Loans"],"summary":"Obtain loan balance details broken down by amount types","description":"

This method provides the outstanding balances on a loan to determine what is owed at this point in time. The resulting structure returns a breakdown of the loan into the following sections:

  • outstandingAmount : Breakdown of principal and fees that makes up the totalOutstandingAmount
  • pastDueAmount : Breakdown of principal and fees that makes up the totalPastDueAmount
  • makeupAmount : Breakdown of principal and fees that makes up the totalMakeupAmount
  • totalOutstandingAmount : This is the money that is expected to be received from the customer as payment on the loan
  • totalPastDueAmount : The total amount of missed payments that have not been paid including any associated fees
  • totalMakeupAmount : Unscheduled past due amounts

The following considerations should be kept in mind when using this method:

  • makeupAmount and totalMakeupAmount does not apply to payday loans

Related Methods

  • Create (schedule) a new payment
","operationId":"Loans_GetLoanBalanceSummary","consumes":[],"produces":["application/json","text/json","text/html","application/xml","text/xml"],"parameters":[{"name":"loanId","in":"path","description":"Unique loan Id. Returned from /loan","required":true,"type":"integer","format":"int32"}],"responses":{"200":{"description":"OK","schema":{"$ref":"#/definitions/LoanBalanceSummaryUIModel"}}},"deprecated":false}},"/api/v1/loans/{loanId}/changeQueue/{queueId}":{"put":{"tags":["Loans"],"summary":"Change the assigned collection queue for a loan","description":"

This method will permit moving a loan from one collection queue to another collection queue.

The following considerations should be kept in mind while using this method:

  • Loan cannot move from one queue to another if a payment arrangement exist on the loan.
  • Loan must be in collection loan status to be eligible to move.
  • Loan can only move to and from following collection queues.

This method permits scheduling a future payment for a collection loan. Collection payments can be posted against any of the two types of calculation mode Manually and Automatically. Current version support only Manually calculation mode. When creating a collection payment the ability to specify amounts for associated fees is available. This method operates at a payment level.

The following considerations should be kept in mind when using this method:

  • A collection payment (paymentCalculationMode=1) is used to schedule a manual collection payment plan. The maximum amount you can schedule is limited by the past due balances that have not been scheduled. When scheduling a collection payment, the following usage restrictions are in place:
    • The date of the collection payment cannot be in the past
    • The paymentModeId cannot be one of the following values:
      • Wire Transfer (paymentModeId = 8)
      • Loan Transfer (paymentModeId = 13)
    • The collection payment total amount of all components has to be less than or equal to the components of the outstanding balance (see GET /api/v1/loans/{loanId}/balance)

Additional Usage Notes

  • This method is only available for collection loans
  • Only one payment can be scheduled at a time
  • To obtain totals or limits for amounts or fees, it is recommend to first invoke the GET /api/v1/loans/{loanId}/balance method and use those values as a reference

Examples

Add a collection manual payment applied to fees and principal

In this example, a customer would like to post a manual collection payment on March 1, 2021. The principal applied is $56.8 and there are $35.56 in fees spread across several fee types. The payment calculation mode was '1'(Manual) and authorization was 'PPD' (authorizationType = 1) and the payment is credit card (paymentModeId = 1).

Example structure:

                      {
                        "PaymentType": 3,
                        "PaymentCalculationMode": 1,
                        "authorizationType": 1,
                        "paymentModeId": 1,
                        "details":
                          {
                          "paymentAmount": 92.36,
                          "principal": 56.8,
                          "fees":
                            [
                              {
                              "feeType": 2,
                              "amount": 10.52
                              },
                              {
                              "feeType": 1,
                              "amount": 5.16
                              },
                              {
                              "feeType": 8,
                              "amount": 2
                              },
                              {
                              "feeType": 3,
                              "amount": 6
                              },
                              {
                              "feeType": 5,
                              "amount": 5.04
                              },
                              {
                              "feeType": 6,
                              "amount": 6.84
                              }
                            ]
                          }
                      }

Related Methods

  • Obtain loan balance details broken down by amount types
  • Delete a payment
  • Change payment due date

This method provides the ability to change the due date of a loan.

The following considerations should be kept in mind when using this method:

  • Changing the loan due date can only be applied to payday/flexpay loans
  • Loans must have a status that is "New Loan" (status = 10), "Pending Application" (status = 2), or "Renewed Loan" (status = 12)
  • Adjustments can only be made within the range defined by the state regulations for this loan
  • Dates cannot be less than today's date
  • Dates must be for a valid portfolio configured business day

Related Methods

  • Change payment due date

This method will permit toggling the status of a specific flag for a loan. This can be used to keep track of activities that have been completed for a loan.

The following considerations should be kept in mind when using this method:

  • The master list of flags that can be set are pulled from the master list of flags enabled on the portfolio
  • The flag status is either enabled or disabled
  • When the status is enabled, the system will automatically record the transition date and time in the flagActivationDate field
  • Toggling the flag status only affects the current loan

  • To save the request code into Loan you must request a verification on UI (LendMate)
  • Only support LendMate (IbvProvider = 1)

Returned from /loan","required":true,"type":"integer","format":"int32"},{"name":"requestCodeModel","in":"body","description":"Json structure with information required to save request code into loan","required":true,"schema":{"$ref":"#/definitions/LoanRequestCodeModel"}}],"responses":{"204":{"description":"No Content"}},"deprecated":false}},"/api/v1/loans/{loanId}/loanagreement":{"get":{"tags":["Loans"],"summary":"Retrieve processed loan agreement","description":"","operationId":"Loans_GetLoanAgreement","consumes":[],"produces":["application/json","text/json","text/html","application/xml","text/xml"],"parameters":[{"name":"loanId","in":"path","description":"Unique loan Id","required":true,"type":"integer","format":"int32"}],"responses":{"200":{"description":"OK","schema":{"$ref":"#/definitions/LoanAgreementUIModel"}}},"deprecated":false}},"/api/v1/loans/{loanId}/loanagreementUrl":{"post":{"tags":["Loans"],"summary":"Retrieve loan agreement URL ","description":"

This endpoint provides a URL that can be used on an iFrame to retrieve, display, and sign the Loan Agreement.

The following considerations should be kept in mind when using this method:

  • The Loan Id provided must exist in the LMS.
  • The Loan must be in Pending Application status.
  • The email and the last 4 digits of the SSN must be associated with the loan.
  • The E-Status must be: Not Signed or Voided.

Once the Loan Agreement is signed:

  • The E-Status will update to Signed.
  • A PDF will be uploaded into the Loan's documents.
  • The E-Status will not change the Loan Status it will remain in Pending Application.
  • The view will automatically log-out.

This method provides the ability to originate a loan.

The following considerations should be kept in mind when using this method:

  • A loan that is to be originated must be in Pending Application status
  • E-sign must be done on the loan before origination
  • Any required check list items must be checked on the loan before origination
  • This call only originates a loan and do not approve the loan if loan approval process is turned on in a portfolio

","operationId":"Loans_OriginateLoan","consumes":["application/json","text/json","text/html","application/xml","text/xml","application/x-www-form-urlencoded"],"produces":[],"parameters":[{"name":"loanId","in":"path","description":"Unique loan Id. Returned from /loan","required":true,"type":"integer","format":"int32"},{"name":"originationModel","in":"body","description":"Json structure with information required to originate a loan","required":true,"schema":{"$ref":"#/definitions/LoanOriginationUIModel"}}],"responses":{"204":{"description":"No Content"}},"deprecated":false}},"/api/v1/loans/{loanId}/paymentMode":{"put":{"tags":["Payments"],"summary":"Update payment mode on a loan","description":"

This method permits updating how money is collected from the customer. This method operates at the loan level and is useful when a customer wants to make a change to a loan (payday, flexpay) or all payments in a loan (installment). To update the payment method for a specific installment payment, see Update payment mode on a payment.

Changing the payment mode requires indicating the new payment mode (paymentModeId) and the authorization type provided by the end user (authorizationType). The following considerations should be kept in mind when using this method:

  • The payment mode can only be changed for active loans in the following statuses:
    • Pending Application (status = 2)
    • New Loan (status = 10)
    • Renewed Loan (status = 12)
    • Pending Collection (status = 38)
  • The payment mode change will only affect unprocessed payments on a loan where the following two conditions are met:
    • The payment status is "Pending" (paymentStatus = 4), or
    • The payment status is "None" (paymentStatus = 5) and the paymentIsBroken element on the payment object is false (paymentIsBroken = false)
  • If one or more payments cannot be updated, none of the payments on the loan will be updated. An error will be returned with details on why the change cannot occur.
  • The payment mode can only be changed to ACH when the following two conditions exist:
    • The current date and time must not be past the ACH cut off time
    • The payment date must be less than or equal to the next business day

Related Methods

  • Update payment mode on a payment
  • Create (schedule) a new payment
  • Change payment due date
  • Delete a payment
  • Update payment mode on a loan

Returned from /loan","required":true,"type":"integer","format":"int32"},{"name":"loanPaymentMode","in":"body","description":"JSON structure containing the payment mode and authorization type enum values","required":true,"schema":{"$ref":"#/definitions/PaymentModeModel"}}],"responses":{"204":{"description":"No Content"}},"deprecated":false}},"/api/v1/loans/{loanId}/payments":{"get":{"tags":["Payments"],"summary":"Returns a collection of PaymentID 's for every payment associated with the provided loan. The maximum value permitted is 100.","required":false,"type":"integer","format":"int32"}],"responses":{"200":{"description":"OK","schema":{"$ref":"#/definitions/PaymentUIPaginationModel"}}},"deprecated":false},"post":{"tags":["Payments"],"summary":"Schedule a payment","description":"

This method permits scheduling a future payment for a customer. The two types of payments supported are makeup payments and additional payments. When creating a makeup payment the ability to specify amounts for associated fees is available. This method operates at a payment level and is only available for installment loans.

The following considerations should be kept in mind when using this method:

  • An additional payment (paymentType=1) is used to schedule an additional payment towards the loan balance. When scheduling an additional payment, the following usage restrictions are in place:
    • The date of the makeup payment cannot be in the past and cannot be beyond due date of the loan
    • The paymentModeId cannot be one of the following values:
      • Wire Transfer (paymentModeId = 8)
      • Loan Transfer (paymentModeId = 13)
  • The amount of the additional payment has to be less than the principal of the outstandingAmount (see GET /api/v1/loans/{loanId}/balance)
  • When isPrincipalOnly is set to true, this indicates the amount should be applied to the principal balance. When set to false it will be distributed to all the loan components.
  • A makeup payment (paymentType=2) is used to schedule full or partial past due amounts for processing. The maximum amount you can schedule is limited by the past due balances that have not been scheduled. When scheduling a makeup payment, the following usage restrictions are in place:
    • The date of the makeup payment cannot be in the past and cannot be beyond due date of the loan
    • The paymentModeId cannot be one of the following values:
      • Wire Transfer (paymentModeId = 8)
      • Loan Transfer (paymentModeId = 13)
    • The makeup payment total amount of all components has to be less than or equal to the components of the makeupAmount balance (see GET /api/v1/loans/{loanId}/balance)

Additional Usage Notes

  • This method is only available for installment loans
  • Only one payment can be scheduled at a time
  • To obtain totals or limits for amounts or fees, it is recommend to first invoke the GET /api/v1/loans/{loanId}/balance method and use those values as a reference

Examples

Add an additional payment that will be applied to principal

In this example, a customer would like to make an additional payment on April 9, 2018 for $30 and have it applied to principal. The authorization was 'PPD' (authorizationType = 1) and the payment is credit card (paymentModeId = 1).

Example structure:

                          {
                            "paymentType": 1,
                            "paymentDate": "2018-04-09",
                            "authorizationType": 1,
                            "paymentModeId": 1,
                            "details": 
                            {
                              "amount": 30,
                              "isPrincipalOnly": true
                            }
                          }
Add a makeup payment applied to principal only with no fees

In this example, a customer would like to make a makeup payment on March 2, 2018 for $56.80 and have it applied to principal. There are no fees associated with this payment. The authorization was 'PPD' (authorizationType = 1) and the payment is ACH (paymentModeId = 2).

Example structure:

                       {
                        "paymentType": 2,
                        "paymentDate": "2018-03-02",
                        "authorizationType": 1,
                        "paymentModeId": 2,
                        "details": 
                        {
                          "principal": 56.8,
                          "fees": []
                        }
                      }
Add a makeup payment applied to fees and principal

In this example, a customer would like to make a makeup payment on March 1, 2018. The principal applied is $56.8 and there are $35.56 in fees spread across several fee types. The authorization was 'PPD' (authorizationType = 1) and the payment is credit card (paymentModeId = 1).

Example structure:

                      {
                        "paymentType": 2,
                        "paymentDate": "2018-03-01",
                        "authorizationType": 1,
                        "paymentModeId": 1,
                        "details": 
                        {
                          "principal": 56.8,
                          "fees": 
                          [
                            {
                              "feeType": 2,
                              "amount": 10.52
                            },
                            {
                              "feeType": 1,
                              "amount": 5.16
                            },
                            {
                              "feeType": 8,
                              "amount": 2
                            },
                            {
                              "feeType": 3,
                              "amount": 6
                            },
                            {
                              "feeType": 5,
                              "amount": 5.04
                            },
                            {
                              "feeType": 6,
                              "amount": 6.84
                            }
                          ]
                        }
                      }

Related Methods

  • Obtain loan balance details broken down by amount types
  • Delete a payment
  • Change payment due date
