Returned from /loan","required":true,"type":"integer","format":"int32"},{"name":"paymentId","in":"path","description":"Payment id","required":true,"type":"integer","format":"int32"}],"responses":{"200":{"description":"OK","schema":{"$ref":"#/definitions/PaymentUIModel"}}},"deprecated":false},"delete":{"tags":["Payments"],"summary":"Delete a payment","description":"

This method provides the ability to delete an unprocessed additional or makeup payment on an installment loan. The following considerations should be kept in mind when using this method:

  • Deleting a payment only applies to installment loans
  • Only additional or makeup payments can be removed
  • If a payoff has been initiated, deleting a payment is not permitted
  • Payments cannot be deleted after the payment date has elapsed
  • If the deleted payment has discounted amounts, when the payment is deleted then the full (non-discounted) amounts are applied back to the loan
  • When deleting a payment, the authorization provided by the user is required (authorizationType)

Related Methods

  • Create (schedule) a new payment
","operationId":"Payments_DeletePayment","consumes":[],"produces":[],"parameters":[{"name":"loanId","in":"path","description":"Unique loan Id","required":true,"type":"integer","format":"int32"},{"name":"paymentId","in":"path","description":"Unique payment Id","required":true,"type":"integer","format":"int32"},{"name":"authorizationType","in":"query","description":"Enumeration to record the authorization provided by the user = ['1','2','3']","required":true,"type":"string"}],"responses":{"204":{"description":"No Content"}},"deprecated":false}},"/api/v1/loans/{loanId}/payments/{paymentId}/dueDate":{"put":{"tags":["Payments"],"summary":"Change payment due date","description":"

This method provides the ability to push a regularly scheduled date forward into the future up to one business day before the next scheduled payment.

The following considerations should be kept in mind when using this method:

  • Adjustments can only be made to the next regularly scheduled payment
  • Adjustments must be between the payment date and one business day before date of the next regularly scheduled payment
  • Dates cannot be less than today's date
  • Dates must be for a valid portfolio configured business day
  • Only for installment loans
  • Only applicable to regularly scheduled payments (other payment types require a delete and recreate)

Related Methods

  • Get payments
  • Schedule a payment
  • Delete a payment
","operationId":"Payments_UpdatePaymentDueDate","consumes":["application/json","text/json","text/html","application/xml","text/xml","application/x-www-form-urlencoded"],"produces":[],"parameters":[{"name":"loanId","in":"path","description":"Unique loan Id. Returned from /loan","required":true,"type":"integer","format":"int32"},{"name":"paymentId","in":"path","description":"Unique payment Id","required":true,"type":"integer","format":"int32"},{"name":"paymentDueDateModel","in":"body","description":"payment due date model","required":true,"schema":{"$ref":"#/definitions/PaymentDueDateModel"}}],"responses":{"204":{"description":"No Content"}},"deprecated":false}},"/api/v1/loans/{loanId}/payments/{paymentId}/paymentMode":{"put":{"tags":["Payments"],"summary":"Update payment mode on a payment","description":"

This method permits updating how money is collected from the customer. This method only operates at a payment level and is useful when a customer wants to make a change for a specific future payment. To update the payment method for an entire loan, see Update payment mode on a loan.

Changing the payment mode requires indicating the new payment mode (paymentModeId) and the authorization type provided by the end user (authorizationType). The following considerations should be kept in mind when using this method:

  • The payment mode can only be changed at a payment level for installment loans.
  • The payment mode change will only affect unprocessed payments on a loan where the following two conditions are met:
    • The payment status is "Pending" (paymentStatus = 4), or
    • The payment status is "None" (paymentStatus = 5) and the paymentIsBroken element on the payment object is false (paymentIsBroken = false)
  • The payment mode can only be changed for originated loans.
  • The payment mode can only be changed to ACH when the following two conditions exist:
    • The current date and time must not be past the ACH cut off time
    • The payment date must be less than or equal to the next business day
  • The payment mode cannot be changed if the existing payment mode is wire transfer (paymentModeId = 8) or loan transfer (paymentModeId = 13)
  • The payment mode cannot be changed to a payment mode of wire transfer (paymentModeId = 8), loan transfer (paymentModeId = 13), or collections ACH (paymentModeId = 9).

Related Methods

  • Update payment mode on a loan
  • Create (schedule) a new payment
  • Change payment due date
  • Delete a payment
  • Update payment mode on a loan
","operationId":"Payments_PutPaymentMode","consumes":["application/json","text/json","text/html","application/xml","text/xml","application/x-www-form-urlencoded"],"produces":[],"parameters":[{"name":"loanId","in":"path","description":"Unique loan Id. Returned from /loan","required":true,"type":"integer","format":"int32"},{"name":"paymentId","in":"path","description":"Unique payment Id","required":true,"type":"integer","format":"int32"},{"name":"paymentMode","in":"body","description":"Payment mode info in json format","required":true,"schema":{"$ref":"#/definitions/PaymentModeModel"}}],"responses":{"204":{"description":"No Content"}},"deprecated":false}},"/api/v1/loans/{loanId}/payments/{paymentId}/postcredittransaction":{"put":{"tags":["Payments"],"summary":"Post a credit payment","description":"

This method permits posting a credit transaction in the system. This intended to mark an existing credit transaction as successful. The following considerations should be kept in mind when using this method:

  • Posting a credit transaction has an specific date restriction:
    • A credit payment can only be posted on the same day the payment is due
  • Posting a credit payment requires that the loan's initial credit payment is pending
  • A payment can only be posted if the payment status is "Pending" (paymentStatus = 4)
","operationId":"Payments_PostSuccessfulCreditPayment","consumes":["application/json","text/json","text/html","application/xml","text/xml","application/x-www-form-urlencoded"],"produces":[],"parameters":[{"name":"loanId","in":"path","description":"Unique loan Id","required":true,"type":"integer","format":"int32"},{"name":"paymentId","in":"path","description":"Unique payment Id","required":true,"type":"integer","format":"int32"},{"name":"postPaymentModel","in":"body","description":"Post payment model","required":true,"schema":{"$ref":"#/definitions/PaymentPutModel"}}],"responses":{"204":{"description":"No Content"}},"deprecated":false}},"/api/v1/loans/{loanId}/payments/{paymentId}/postpayment":{"put":{"tags":["Payments"],"summary":"Post a debit payment","description":"

This method permits posting a credit transaction in the system. This intended to mark an existing credit transaction as successful. The following considerations should be kept in mind when using this method:

  • Posting a credit transaction has an specific date restriction:
    • A credit payment can only be posted on the same day the payment is due
  • Posting a credit payment requires that the loan's initial credit payment is pending
  • A payment can only be posted if the payment status is "Pending" (paymentStatus = 4)
","operationId":"Payments_PostSuccessfulPayment","consumes":["application/json","text/json","text/html","application/xml","text/xml","application/x-www-form-urlencoded"],"produces":[],"parameters":[{"name":"loanId","in":"path","description":"Unique loan Id","required":true,"type":"integer","format":"int32"},{"name":"paymentId","in":"path","description":"Unique payment Id","required":true,"type":"integer","format":"int32"},{"name":"postPaymentModel","in":"body","description":"Post payment model","required":true,"schema":{"$ref":"#/definitions/PaymentPutModel"}}],"responses":{"204":{"description":"No Content"}},"deprecated":false}},"/api/v1/loans/{loanId}/payments/RemainingNumberOfPayments":{"get":{"tags":["Payments"],"summary":"Returns the remaining number of payments of a specified loan ","description":"

This methods returns the remaining number of payments on a specified loan

  • A count for all regular payments in payment schedule with a payment status = none. This function will not take into consideration additional and makeup payments
  • In-Collections Loans (returned item or charge off status) will return the amount of remaining Settlement or Collection payments
","operationId":"Payments_GetLoanRemainingPayments","consumes":[],"produces":["application/json","text/json","text/html","application/xml","text/xml"],"parameters":[{"name":"loanId","in":"path","description":"Unique loan Id. Returned from /loan","required":true,"type":"integer","format":"int32"}],"responses":{"200":{"description":"OK","schema":{"$ref":"#/definitions/RemainingNumberOfPayments"}}},"deprecated":false}},"/api/v1/loans/{loanId}/payoff":{"get":{"tags":["Loans"],"summary":"Returns the payoff information for a loan","description":"","operationId":"Loans_GetLoanPayoff","consumes":[],"produces":["application/json","text/json","text/html","application/xml","text/xml"],"parameters":[{"name":"loanId","in":"path","description":"Unique loan Id","required":true,"type":"integer","format":"int32"},{"name":"payOffDate","in":"query","description":"The date of when the loan will be paid off in the format YYYY-MM-DD","required":true,"type":"string"}],"responses":{"200":{"description":"OK","schema":{"$ref":"#/definitions/PayoffUIGetModel"}}},"deprecated":false},"post":{"tags":["Loans"],"summary":"Schedule a payoff","description":"

This method will permit scheduling a final payoff to transition a loan to closed status.

The following considerations should be kept in mind when using this method:

  • A payoff can only be scheduled for a loan in good standing (a loan that has been originated that is not in collections).
  • This method only applies to installment loans.
  • This functionality will schedule a payment representing the current balance for the loan. The amount will be the same as the value obtained from the GET payoff method.
  • The date of the scheduled payoff must be between today (non-ACH) or next business day (ACH) and one day prior to the last payment date.

Related Methods

  • Get payoff information
","operationId":"Loans_PostPayoff","consumes":["application/json","text/json","text/html","application/xml","text/xml","application/x-www-form-urlencoded"],"produces":["application/json","text/json","text/html","application/xml","text/xml"],"parameters":[{"name":"loanId","in":"path","description":"Unique loan Id","required":true,"type":"integer","format":"int32"},{"name":"payoff","in":"body","description":"payoff post UI model","required":true,"schema":{"$ref":"#/definitions/PayoffPostUIModel"}}],"responses":{"200":{"description":"OK","schema":{"$ref":"#/definitions/PayoffUIGetModel"}}},"deprecated":false},"delete":{"tags":["Loans"],"summary":"Remove a scheduled payoff","description":"

This method will permit removing a scheduled payoff.

The following considerations should be kept in mind when using this method:

  • Rolling back a payoff can only occur on a loan that has an unprocessed payoff scheduled.
  • Rolling back a payoff will restore the previous payment schedule.

Related Methods

  • Return payoff information for a loan
  • Schedule a payoff
","operationId":"Loans_RollbackPayoff","consumes":[],"produces":[],"parameters":[{"name":"loanId","in":"path","description":"Unique loan Id","required":true,"type":"integer","format":"int32"},{"name":"authorizationType","in":"query","description":"Enumeration to record the authorization provided by the user = ['1','2','3']","required":true,"type":"string"}],"responses":{"204":{"description":"No Content"}},"deprecated":false}},"/api/v1/loans/{loanId}/pendingApplication/recalculation":{"put":{"tags":["Loans"],"summary":"Change program, interest rate, approved principal amount, number of installments on a loan","description":"

This method permits changing a program, principal amount, interest rate and number of installments on a particular loan in Pending Application status only.

The following considerations should be kept in mind when using this method:

  • If multiple parameters are applied for update and result is an error then no changes are applied on loan at all.
  • Change in any of these parameters will trigger payment schedule recalculation on the loan.
  • Parameter values must be in range of store settings for update to be successfully applied
  • This call applies to installment loans only.

Related Methods

  • Rescind a loan
  • Void a loan

This method provides the ability to renew a loan.

The following considerations should be kept in mind when using this method:

  • Loans must have a status that is "New Loan" (status = 10) or "Renewed Loan" (paymentStatus = 12)
  • A renew action can only be applied to a payday or flexpay loan
  • Loan can only be renewed before the due date of the loan
  • For a renew to occur, the loan must meet the state regulations defined in the system

Related Methods

  • Rescind a loan
  • Void a loan
","operationId":"Loans_RenewLoan","consumes":[],"produces":[],"parameters":[{"name":"loanId","in":"path","description":"Unique loan ID. Returned from /loan","required":true,"type":"integer","format":"int32"}],"responses":{"204":{"description":"No Content"}},"deprecated":false}},"/api/v1/loans/{loanId}/rescind":{"get":{"tags":["Loans"],"summary":"Return number of days available for a rescind","description":"

This method provides information on when a rescind can be performed on a loan. It will return the number of days remaining (rescindDaysRemaining) that a rescind can be performed.

The following considerations should be kept in mind when using this method:

  • The value of rescindDaysRemaining is expressed in number of calendar days remaining for a rescind to occur
  • A value of zero (0) indicates today is the last day the loan can be rescinded
  • A value of negative one (-1) indicates a rescind cannot occur

Related Methods

  • Rescind a loan
","operationId":"Loans_GetDaysLeftToRescindLoan","consumes":[],"produces":["application/json","text/json","text/html","application/xml","text/xml"],"parameters":[{"name":"loanId","in":"path","description":"Unique loan ID. Returned from /loans","required":true,"type":"integer","format":"int32"}],"responses":{"200":{"description":"OK","schema":{"$ref":"#/definitions/LoanRescindDaysLeftUIModel"}}},"deprecated":false},"put":{"tags":["Loans"],"summary":"Rescind a Loan","description":"

This method provides the ability to rescind a loan.

The following considerations should be kept in mind when using this method:

  • Must be an originated loan with a successful credit
  • A rescind is only valid in either of the following two conditions:
    • Must be before the first payment
    • Must be within the maximum number of days for rescind defined in the state regulations
  • A rescind can only be applied to installment loans

Related Methods

  • Return number of days available for a rescind
  • Renew a loan
  • Void a loan

This method will return summary information of customer, payments, signature data in addition to the loan information for a particular loan.

The following consideration should be kept in mind when using this method:

  • The resulting structure returns only loan information if all filters are off
  • To see information about customer, payments, signature, the respective filters should be turned on.

This method will permit voiding a loan.

The following considerations should be kept in mind when using this method:

  • Only the following void reasons are permitted to be used during a void:
    • Set up in error (voidReason = 1)
    • Invalid e-signature date (voidReason = 2)
    • Client refused (voidReason = 3)
  • The loan must be in a new loan status (status = 10)
  • The loan cannot have already sent out the credit payment.
  • The loan cannot be a transferred loan (isLoanTransfer = false)
  • The loan cannot be an expedited loan (expeditedPaymentType = 0)

Related Methods

  • Rescind a loan
  • Renew a loan
  • Payoff a loan
